title: cycnium serratum
intro: Cycnium serratum, also known as the blue toadflax, is a poisonous plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Cycnium serratum can be prepared as a tincture or infused in oil for herbal remedies, although it should be used with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional due to its toxicity.
